english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Mushir -> Ibn Jurayj -> Abu al-Zubayr -> Jabir
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Mushir narrated to us from Ibn Jurayj, from Abu al-Zubayr, from Jabir, who said: "The Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed Tawaf around the House during the Farewell Hajj on his mount, touching the Stone with his staff."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ خَالِدٍ الْحَذَّاءِ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ طَافَ بِالْبَيْتِ عَلَى بَعِيرٍ، فَكَانَ إِذَا أَتَى عَلَى الْحَجَرِ الْأَسْوَدِ أَشَارَ إِلَيْهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn 'Ulayyah -> Khalid al-Hadhdha' -> 'Ikrimah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn 'Ulayyah narrated to us from Khalid al-Hadhdha', from 'Ikrimah, "that the Prophet ﷺ performed Tawaf around the House on a camel, and whenever he came to the Black Stone, he pointed to it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا عَبْدَةُ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، أَنَّ أُمَّ سَلَمَةَ قَالَتْ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، مَا طُفْنَا طَوَافَ الْخُرُوجِ، فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: إِذَا أُقِيمَتِ الصَّلَاةُ، فَطُوفِي عَلَى بَعِيرِكَ، مِنْ وَرَاءِ النَّاسِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Abdah -> Hisham -> His Father
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: 'Abdah narrated to us from Hisham, from his father, that Umm Salamah said: "O Messenger of Allah, we have not performed the Farewell Tawaf." The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "When the prayer is established, perform Tawaf on your camel behind the people."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ يَزِيدَ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: جَاءَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ وَقَدْ اشْتَكَى، فَطَافَ بِالْبَيْتِ عَلَى بَعِيرٍ، وَمَعَهُ مِحْجَنٌ، كُلَّمَا مَرَّ عَلَى الْحَجَرِ، اسْتَلَمَهُ، فَلَمَّا فَرَغَ مِنْ طَوَافِهِ أَنَاخَ ثُمَّ صَلَّى رَكْعَتَيْنِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Fudayl -> Yazid -> 'Ikrimah -> Ibn 'Abbas
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Fudayl narrated to us from Yazid, from 'Ikrimah, from Ibn 'Abbas, who said: "The Messenger of Allah ﷺ came while he was complaining of pain, so he performed Tawaf around the House on a camel, carrying a staff. Whenever he passed by the Stone, he touched it. When he finished his Tawaf, he made his camel kneel and prayed two Rak'ahs."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ مَعْرُوفٍ الْمَكِّيِّ قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا الطُّفَيْلِ وَأَنَا غُلَامٌ وَقَالَ: رَأَيْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يَطُوفُ بِالْبَيْتِ عَلَى رَاحِلَتِهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Ma'ruf al-Makki
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki' narrated to us from Ma'ruf al-Makki, who said: I heard Abu al-Tufayl while I was a boy saying: "I saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ performing Tawaf around the House on his mount."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا أَبُو خَالِدٍ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ طَافَ بِالْبَيْتِ عَلَى رَاحِلَتِهِ يَسْتَلِمُ الْحَجَرَ بِمِحْجَنِهِ وَبَيْنَ الصَّفَا وَالْمَرْوَةِ فَقُلْتُ لِعَطَاءٍ: مَا أَرَادَ إِلَى ذَلِكَ قَالَ: التَّوْسِعَةَ عَلَى أُمَّتِهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu Khalid -> Hajjaj -> 'Ata'
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abu Khalid narrated to us from Hajjaj, from 'Ata', "that the Prophet ﷺ performed Tawaf around the House on his mount, touching the Stone with his staff, and between Safa and Marwah." I said to 'Ata': "What did he intend by that?" He said: "To make things easier for his nation."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ عُرْوَةَ قَالَ: كَانَ أَبِي، رَآهُمْ يَطُوفُونَ بِالْبَيْتِ عَلَى الدَّوَابِّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu Usamah -> Hisham ibn 'Urwah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abu Usamah narrated to us from Hisham ibn 'Urwah, who said: My father "saw them performing Tawaf around the House on animals."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ حَمَّادٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ سَعَى عَلَى رَاحِلَتِهِ بَيْنَ الصَّفَا وَالْمَرْوَةِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Sufyan -> Hammad -> Sa'id ibn Jubayr
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki' narrated to us from Sufyan, from Hammad, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, "that the Prophet ﷺ performed Sa'y on his mount between Safa and Marwah."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ قَيْسِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، عَنْ أَبِي إِدْرِيسَ قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ عَائِشَةَ تَسْعَى بَيْنَ الصَّفَا وَالْمَرْوَةِ عَلَى بَغْلٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Qays ibn 'Abdullah -> Abu Idris
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki' narrated to us from Qays ibn 'Abdullah, from Abu Idris, who said: "I saw 'Aishah performing Sa'y between Safa and Marwah on a mule."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنِ الْأَحْوَصِ قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ أَنَسًا يَطُوفُ بَيْنَ الصَّفَا وَالْمَرْوَةِ عَلَى حِمَارٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Muhammad ibn Fudayl -> Al-Ahwas
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Muhammad ibn Fudayl narrated to us from Al-Ahwas, who said: "I saw Anas performing Tawaf between Safa and Marwah on a donkey."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا ابْنُ نُمَيْرٍ، عَنِ الرَّبِيعِ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ، عَنِ الطَّوَافِ بَيْنَ الصَّفَا وَالْمَرْوَةِ، فَقَالَ: طَافَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ رَاكِبًا، وَأَنَا أَطُوفُ رَاكِبًا، فَطُفْتُ أَنَا وَهُوَ رَاكِبَيْنِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Numayr -> Al-Rabi' ibn Sa'id
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Numayr narrated to us from Al-Rabi' ibn Sa'id, who said: I asked Abu Ja'far about Tawaf between Safa and Marwah. He said: "The Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed Tawaf riding, and I perform Tawaf riding." So I and he performed Tawaf riding.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، وَعَطَاءٍ، أَنَّهُمَا كَانَا يَكْرَهَانِ رُكُوبَ الرِّجَالِ وَالنِّسَاءِ بَيْنَ الصَّفَا وَالْمَرْوَةِ إِلَّا مِنْ عُذْرٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Abd al-A'la -> Hisham -> Al-Hasan and 'Ata'
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: 'Abd al-A'la narrated to us from Hisham, from Al-Hasan and 'Ata', "that they disliked men and women riding between Safa and Marwah unless there was an excuse."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا ابْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ خَارِجَةَ بْنِ الْحَارِثِ قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ عِرَاكَ بْنَ مَالِكٍ، يَطُوفُ بَيْنَ الصَّفَا وَالْمَرْوَةِ عَلَى حِمَارٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Mahdi -> Kharijah ibn al-Harith
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Mahdi narrated to us from Kharijah ibn al-Harith, who said: "I saw 'Irak ibn Malik performing Tawaf between Safa and Marwah on a donkey."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا الْفَضْلُ بْنُ دُكَيْنٍ، عَنْ يَزِيدَ الشَّيْبَانِيِّ قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ مُجَاهِدًا وَعَطَاءً يَسْعَيَانِ بَيْنَ الصَّفَا وَالْمَرْوَةِ عَلَى دَابَّتَيْنِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Al-Fadl ibn Dukayn -> Yazid al-Shaybani
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Al-Fadl ibn Dukayn narrated to us from Yazid al-Shaybani, who said: "I saw Mujahid and 'Ata' performing Sa'y between Safa and Marwah on two animals."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا ابْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ عُرْوَةَ قَالَ: كَانَ أَبِي إِذَا رَآهُمْ يَسْعَوْنَ بَيْنَ الصَّفَا وَالْمَرْوَةِ رُكْبَانًا قَالَ: قَدْ خَابَ هَؤُلَاءِ وَخَسِرُوا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Idris -> Hisham ibn 'Urwah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Idris narrated to us from Hisham ibn 'Urwah, who said: My father used to say when he saw people performing Sa'y between Safa and Marwah while riding: "These people have failed and lost."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا ابْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ زَمْعَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ طَاوُسٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، أَنَّهُ يَكْرَهُ الرُّكُوبَ بَيْنَ الصَّفَا وَالْمَرْوَةِ إِلَّا مِنْ ضَرُورَةٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Mahdi -> Zam'ah -> Ibn Tawus -> His Father
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Mahdi narrated to us from Zam'ah, from Ibn Tawus, from his father, "that he disliked riding between Safa and Marwah except out of necessity."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا أَبُو الْأَحْوَصِ، عَنْ أَبِي يَعْفُورٍ قَالَ: خَطَبَنَا رَجُلٌ مِنْ خُزَاعَةَ كَانَ أَمِيرًا عَلَى الْحَجِّ بِمَكَّةَ، فَقَالَ: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ، إِنَّ عُمَرَ كَانَ رَجُلًا شَدِيدًا، وَإِنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ قَالَ لَهُ: يَا عُمَرُ، إِنَّكَ رَجُلٌ شَدِيدٌ، تُؤْذِي الضَّعِيفَ، فَإِذَا طُفْتَ بِالْبَيْتِ، وَرَأَيْتَ مِنَ الْحَجَرِ خَلْوَةً، فَادْنُ مِنْهُ، وَإِلَّا فَكَبِّرْ، وَهَلِّلْ، وَامْضِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu al-Ahwas -> Abu Ya'fur
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abu al-Ahwas narrated to us from Abu Ya'fur, who said: A man from Khuza'ah, who was the emir of Hajj in Makkah, addressed us saying: "O people, 'Umar was a strong man, and the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to him: 'O 'Umar, you are a strong man, do not harm the weak. When you perform Tawaf around the House and see a space at the Stone, approach it; otherwise, say Takbir and Tahlil and proceed.'"

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: إِذَا حَاذَيْتَ بِهِ، فَكَبِّرْ، وَادْعُ، وَصَلِّ عَلَى النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Fudayl -> Hajjaj -> 'Ata' -> Ibn 'Abbas
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Fudayl narrated to us from Hajjaj, from 'Ata', from Ibn 'Abbas, who said: "When you align with it (the Stone), say Takbir, supplicate, and send blessings upon the Prophet ﷺ."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ عَاصِمٍ قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ أَنَسَ بْنَ مَالِكٍ يَطُوفُ بِالْبَيْتِ، حَتَّى إِذَا حَاذَى بِالْحَجَرِ، نَظَرَ إِلَيْهِ، وَالْتَفَتَ إِلَيْهِ، فَكَبَّرَ نَحْوَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Hafs -> 'Asim
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Hafs narrated to us from 'Asim, who said: "I saw Anas ibn Malik performing Tawaf around the House. When he aligned with the Stone, he looked at it, turned towards it, and said Takbir facing it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ عَاصِمٍ قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ أَنَسًا يَسْتَقْبِلُ الْأَرْكَانَ بِالتَّكْبِيرِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Fudayl -> 'Asim
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Fudayl narrated to us from 'Asim, who said: "I saw Anas facing the corners with Takbir."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا عَبْدَةُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، أَنَّ هِشَامَ بْنَ عُرْوَةَ قَالَ: كَانَ أَبِي، إِذَا غُلِبَ اسْتَقْبَلَهُ وَكَبَّرَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Abdah ibn Sulayman -> Hisham ibn 'Urwah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: 'Abdah ibn Sulayman narrated that Hisham ibn 'Urwah said: My father "would face it and say Takbir if he was overcome (by the crowd)."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ سَعِيدَ بْنَ جُبَيْرٍ حِينَ اسْتَفْتَحَ الطَّوَافَ اسْتَقْبَلَ الْحَجَرَ وَلَمْ يَتْرُكْهُ، وَرَفَعَ يَدَيْهِ وَكَبَّرَ، فَسَأَلْتُ عَطَاءً فَقَالَ: كَبِّرْ وَلَا تَرْفَعْ يَدَيْكَ بِالتَّكْبِيرِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Fudayl -> 'Abd al-Malik
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Fudayl narrated to us from 'Abd al-Malik, who said: I saw Sa'id ibn Jubayr when he began Tawaf facing the Stone and not leaving it, raising his hands and saying Takbir. I asked 'Ata', and he said: "Say Takbir but do not raise your hands with the Takbir."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ بُرْجَانَ قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ مُجَاهِدًا إِذَا مَرَّ بِالْحَجَرِ نَظَرَ إِلَيْهِ، فَكَبَّرَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Muhammad ibn Burjan
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki' narrated to us from Muhammad ibn Burjan, who said: "I saw Mujahid looking at the Stone and saying Takbir whenever he passed by it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، وَوَكِيعٌ، عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ عُرْوَةَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ: قَالَ: قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ لِعَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ عَوْفٍ: مَا صَنَعْتَ؟ قَالَ: اسْتَلَمْتُ وَتَرَكْتُ، قَالَ: أَصَبْتَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Fudayl and Waki' -> Hisham ibn 'Urwah -> His Father
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Fudayl and Waki' narrated to us from Hisham ibn 'Urwah, from his father, who said: The Prophet ﷺ said to 'Abd al-Rahman ibn 'Awf: "What did you do?" He said: "I touched and left." He said: "You did right."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ طَلْحَةَ بْنِ يَحْيَى، عَنِ الْقَاسِمِ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ ابْنَ عُمَرَ زَاحَمَ عَلَى الْحَجَرِ حَتَّى دَمِيَ مَنْخِرُهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Talhah ibn Yahya -> Al-Qasim
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki' narrated to us from Talhah ibn Yahya, from Al-Qasim, who said: "I saw Ibn 'Umar crowding at the Stone until his nose bled."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا عَلِيُّ بْنُ مُسْهِرٍ، عَنِ الشَّيْبَانِيِّ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، قَالَ: طُفْتُ مَعَهُ، فَكَانَ لَا يُزَاحِمُ عَلَى الْحَجَرِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Ali ibn Mushir -> Al-Shaybani -> Sa'id ibn Jubayr
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: 'Ali ibn Mushir narrated to us from Al-Shaybani, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, who said: "I performed Tawaf with him, and he would not crowd at the Stone."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا أَبُو سَعِيدٍ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مَيْسَرَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ قَالَ: إِذَا كَانَ عَلَى الْحَجَرِ زِحَامٌ، فَلَا تُؤْذِيَنَّ، وَابْعُدْ مِنْهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu Sa'id Muhammad ibn Maysarah -> Ibn Jurayj -> 'Ata'
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abu Sa'id Muhammad ibn Maysarah narrated to us from Ibn Jurayj, from 'Ata', who said: "If there is crowding at the Stone, do not harm anyone, and keep away from it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا وَكِيعٌ، عَنِ الْمُخْتَارِ أَبِي عَمْرٍو، عَنْ جَابِرِ بْنِ زَيْدٍ، قَالَ: لَا يُزَاحَمُ عَلَى الْحَجَرِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Al-Mukhtar Abu 'Amr -> Jabir ibn Zayd
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki' narrated to us from Al-Mukhtar Abu 'Amr, from Jabir ibn Zayd, who said: "One should not crowd at the Stone."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: كَانَ يَكْرَهُ أَنْ يُزَاحِمَ عَلَى الْحَجَرِ، تُؤْذِي مُسْلِمًا، أَوْ يُؤْذِيكَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Fudayl -> Hajjaj -> 'Ata' -> Ibn 'Abbas
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Fudayl narrated to us from Hajjaj, from 'Ata', from Ibn 'Abbas, who said: "He disliked crowding at the Stone, harming a Muslim or being harmed."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا عُبَيْدُ اللَّهِ، عَنْ إِسْرَائِيلَ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، وَمُجَاهِدٍ، وَمُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ، وَسَالِمٍ، وَالْقَاسِمِ أَنَّهُمْ لَمْ يَكُونُوا يُزَاحِمُونَ عَلَى الْحَجَرِ، وَكَانُوا يُقِيمُونَ سَاعَةً مُسْتَقْبَلَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Ubayd Allah -> Isra'il -> Jabir -> 'Ata', Mujahid, Muhammad ibn 'Ali, Salim, and Al-Qasim
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: 'Ubayd Allah narrated to us from Isra'il, from Jabir, from 'Ata', Mujahid, Muhammad ibn 'Ali, Salim, and Al-Qasim, "that they did not crowd at the Stone, but would stand facing it for a while."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا الْفَضْلُ بْنُ دُكَيْنٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ عُبَيْدٍ الطَّائِيِّ قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ الْحَسَنَ أَتَى الْحَجَرَ، فَرَأَى زِحَامًا، فَلَمْ يَسْتَلِمْهُ، فَدَعَا، ثُمَّ أَتَى الْمَقَامَ، فَصَلَّى عِنْدَهُ رَكْعَتَيْنِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Al-Fadl ibn Dukayn -> Sa'id ibn 'Ubayd al-Ta'i
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Al-Fadl ibn Dukayn narrated to us from Sa'id ibn 'Ubayd al-Ta'i, who said: "I saw Al-Hasan come to the Stone, but seeing a crowd, he did not touch it. He supplicated, then went to the Maqam and prayed two Rak'ahs there."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ أَبِي الْعَوَّامِ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَسْتَلِمُهُ، وَلَا يُزَاحِمُ، وَكَانَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ يَفْعَلُهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Abu al-'Awwam -> 'Ata' -> Ibn 'Abbas
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki' narrated to us from Abu al-'Awwam, from 'Ata', from Ibn 'Abbas, "that he used to touch it but not crowd." And Ibn 'Umar used to do the same.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا حَفْصٌ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، وَحَجَّاجٍ، وَعَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ ابْنَ عَبَّاسٍ يَقُولُ: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ، إِنَّ دُخُولَكُمُ الْبَيْتَ لَيْسَ مِنْ حَجِّكُمْ فِي شَيْءٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Hafs -> Ibn Jurayj, Hajjaj, and 'Abd al-Malik -> 'Ata'
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Hafs narrated to us from Ibn Jurayj, Hajjaj, and 'Abd al-Malik, from 'Ata', who said: I heard Ibn 'Abbas say: "O people, entering the House is not part of your Hajj at all."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، فِي الْحَاجِّ قَالَ: إِنْ شَاءَ دَخَلَ الْكَعْبَةَ، وَإِنْ شَاءَ لَمْ يَدْخُلْهَا وَقَالَ: إِنْ دَخَلَهَا فَحَسَنٌ وَإِنْ لَمْ يَدْخُلْهَا فَلَا بَأْسَ، وَإِنْ دَخَلْتَهَا فَتَنَافَسْ إِلَى السَّارِيَةِ الْوُسْطَى، فَصَلِّ عِنْدَهَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Jarir -> Mughirah -> Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Jarir narrated to us from Mughirah, from Ibrahim, regarding the pilgrim. He said: "If he wishes, he enters the Ka'bah, and if he wishes, he does not enter it." He said: "If he enters it, it is good, and if he does not enter it, there is no harm. And if you enter it, hasten to the middle pillar and pray there."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنِ الْعَلَاءِ، عَنْ خَيْثَمَةَ، أَنَّهُ سُئِلَ عَنْ دُخُولِ الْبَيْتِ فَقَالَ: لَا يَضُرُّكَ وَاللَّهِ أَنْ لَا تَدْخُلَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Fudayl -> Al-'Ala' -> Khaythamah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Fudayl narrated to us from Al-'Ala', from Khaythamah, that he was asked about entering the House. He said: "By Allah, it does not harm you if you do not enter it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ وَاقِدٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ قَالَ: إِنْ شِئْتَ فَلَا تَدْخُلْهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Waqid -> 'Ata'
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki' narrated to us from Waqid, from 'Ata', who said: "If you wish, do not enter it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ قَالَ: مَنْ دَخَلَ الْبَيْتَ دَخَلَ فِي حَسَنَةٍ، وَخَرَجَ مِنْ سَيِّئَةٍ، وَخَرَجَ مَغْفُورًا لَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Jarir -> Layth -> Mujahid
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Jarir narrated to us from Layth, from Mujahid, who said: "Whoever enters the House enters into goodness, exits from evil, and comes out forgiven."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا سُفْيَانُ بْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، عَنْ عُرْوَةَ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ قَالَتْ: حَاضَتْ صَفِيَّةُ بَعْدَمَا أَفَاضَتْ، فَأُخْبِرَ بِذَلِكَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فَقَالَ: أَحَابِسَتُنَا هِيَ؟ قُلْتُ: قَدْ طَافَتْ ثُمَّ حَاضَتْ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ، قَالَ: فَلْتَنْفِرْ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Sufyan ibn 'Uyaynah -> Al-Zuhri -> 'Urwah -> 'Aishah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Sufyan ibn 'Uyaynah narrated to us from Al-Zuhri, from 'Urwah, from 'Aishah, who said: Safiyyah menstruated after she had performed Tawaf al-Ifadah. The Prophet ﷺ was informed of that, and he said: "Is she detaining us?" I said: "She has performed Tawaf, then menstruated after that." He said: "Then let her depart."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا ابْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ الْقَاسِمِ التَّيْمِيِّ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، بِمِثْلِهِ، إِلَّا أَنَّهُ قَالَ: فَلَا إِذًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn 'Uyaynah -> 'Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Qasim al-Taymi -> His Father -> 'Aishah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn 'Uyaynah narrated to us from 'Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Qasim al-Taymi, from his father, from 'Aishah, similarly, except that he said: "Then no (she is not detaining us)."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، عَنِ الْأَسْوَدِ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ قَالَتْ: ذَكَرَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ صَفِيَّةَ فَقُلْتُ: إِنَّهَا قَدْ حَاضَتْ قَالَ: عَقْرَى حَلْقَى مَا أُرَاهَا إِلَّا حَابِسَتَنَا قَالَتْ: قُلْتُ: إِنَّهَا قَدْ طَافَتْ يَوْمَ النَّحْرِ قَالَ: فَلَا إِذًا مُرُوهَا فَلْتَنْفِرْ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu Mu'awiyah -> Al-A'mash -> Ibrahim -> Al-Aswad -> 'Aishah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abu Mu'awiyah narrated to us from Al-A'mash, from Ibrahim, from Al-Aswad, from 'Aishah, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ mentioned Safiyyah, and I said: "She has menstruated." He said: "Aqra Halqa! I think she will detain us." She said: I said: "She has performed Tawaf on the Day of Sacrifice." He said: "Then no (she is not detaining us). Tell her to depart."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ أَبِي فَرْوَةَ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ قَاسِمَ بْنَ مُحَمَّدٍ، عَنِ امْرَأَةٍ زَارَتِ الْبَيْتَ يَوْمَ النَّحْرِ، ثُمَّ حَاضَتْ يَوْمَ النَّحْرِ، فَقَالَ: يَرْحَمُ اللَّهُ عُمَرَ، قَالَ أَصْحَابُ مُحَمَّدٍ: قَدْ فَرَغَتْ، إِلَّا عُمَرَ فَإِنَّهُ كَانَ يَقُولُ: يَكُونُ آخِرَ عَهْدِهَا بِالْبَيْتِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Jarir -> Abu Farwah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Jarir narrated to us from Abu Farwah, who said: I asked Qasim ibn Muhammad about a woman who visited the House on the Day of Sacrifice and then menstruated on the Day of Sacrifice. He said: "May Allah have mercy on 'Umar. The Companions of Muhammad said: 'She has finished,' except 'Umar, for he used to say: 'Her last act should be at the House.'"

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ مِسْعَرٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ بْنِ مَيْسَرَةَ، عَنْ طَاوُسٍ قَالَ: مَا رَأَيْتُ ابْنَ عَبَّاسٍ خَالَفَهُ أَحَدٌ فِي شَيْءٍ فَتَرَكَهُ حَتَّى يُقَرِّرَهُ، فَخَالَفَهُ جَابِرُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ فِي الْمَرْأَةِ تَطُوفُ ثُمَّ تَحِيضُ، فَقَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ: تَنْفِرُ، فَأَرْسَلُوا إِلَى امْرَأَةٍ كَانَ أَصَابَهَا ذَلِكَ، فَوَافَقَتْ ابْنَ عَبَّاسٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Mis'ar -> 'Abd al-Malik ibn Maysarah -> Tawus
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki' narrated to us from Mis'ar, from 'Abd al-Malik ibn Maysarah, from Tawus, who said: "I never saw anyone disagree with Ibn 'Abbas on anything except that he would leave it until he established it. Jabir ibn 'Abdullah disagreed with him regarding a woman who performs Tawaf and then menstruates. Ibn 'Abbas said: 'She departs.' So they sent to a woman who had experienced that, and she agreed with Ibn 'Abbas."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ يَعْلَى بْنِ عَطَاءٍ، قَالَ: سَمِعَ الْقَاسِمَ بْنَ رَبِيعَةَ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ سَعْدَ بْنَ مَالِكٍ، عَنْ امْرَأَةٍ حَاضَتْ بَعْدَ الطَّوَافِ بِالْبَيْتِ يَوْمَ النَّحْرِ، قَالَ: تَصْدُرُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ghundar -> Shu'bah -> Ya'la ibn 'Ata'
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ghundar narrated to us from Shu'bah, from Ya'la ibn 'Ata', who heard Al-Qasim ibn Rabi'ah say: I asked Sa'd ibn Malik about a woman who menstruated after Tawaf around the House on the Day of Sacrifice. He said: "She departs."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا جَرِيرٌ، عَنِ ابْنِ شُبْرُمَةَ، عَنْ سَالِمِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ يُقِيمُ عَلَى الْحَائِضِ، فَإِنْ كَانَتْ طَافَتْ طَوَافَ يَوْمِ النَّحْرِ، سَبْعَةَ أَيَّامٍ، حَتَّى تَطُوفَ طَوَافَ يَوْمِ النَّفْرِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Jarir -> Ibn Shubrumah -> Salim ibn 'Abdullah -> His Father
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Jarir narrated to us from Ibn Shubrumah, from Salim ibn 'Abdullah, from his father, "that he used to wait for a menstruating woman, if she had performed the Tawaf of the Day of Sacrifice, for seven days until she could perform the Tawaf of the Day of Departure."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ إِسْرَائِيلَ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ يَزِيدَ بْنِ هَانِئٍ، أَنَّ امْرَأَةً طَافَتْ، ثُمَّ حَاضَتْ يَوْمَ النَّحْرِ، بَعْدَمَا طَافَتْ، فَسُئِلَ الْحَسَنُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ فَقَالَ: تَنْفِرُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Isra'il -> Abu Ishaq -> Yazid ibn Hani'
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki' narrated to us from Isra'il, from Abu Ishaq, from Yazid ibn Hani', that a woman performed Tawaf, then menstruated on the Day of Sacrifice after she had performed Tawaf. Al-Hasan ibn 'Ali was asked, and he said: "She departs."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا عَفَّانُ، قَالَ: ثنا أَبُو عَوَانَةَ، عَنْ يَعْلَى بْنِ عَطَاءٍ، عَنِ الْوَلِيدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، عَنِ الْحَارِثِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَوْسٍ الثَّقَفِيِّ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ عُمَرَ بْنَ الْخَطَّابِ عَنِ الْمَرْأَةِ تَطُوفُ بِالْبَيْتِ، ثُمَّ تَحِيضُ، فَقَالَ: آخِرُ عَهْدِهَا بِالْبَيْتِ، فَقَالَ الْحَارِثُ: كَذَلِكَ أَفْتَانِي رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فَقَالَ عُمَرُ: أُرِبْتَ عَنْ يَدَيْكَ، سَأَلْتنِي عَنْ شَيْءٍ سَأَلْتَ عَنْهُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ كَيْمَا أُخَالِفَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Affan -> Abu 'Awanah -> Ya'la ibn 'Ata' -> Al-Walid ibn 'Abd al-Rahman -> Al-Harith ibn 'Abdullah ibn Aws al-Thaqafi
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: 'Affan narrated to us, saying: Abu 'Awanah narrated to us from Ya'la ibn 'Ata', from Al-Walid ibn 'Abd al-Rahman, from Al-Harith ibn 'Abdullah ibn Aws al-Thaqafi, who said: I asked 'Umar ibn al-Khattab about a woman who performs Tawaf around the House and then menstruates. He said: "Her last act should be at the House." Al-Harith said: "That is how the Messenger of Allah ﷺ instructed me." 'Umar said: "May your hands be tied! You asked me about something you had asked the Messenger of Allah ﷺ about so that I might contradict him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنُ يُونُسَ قَالَ: ثنا أَبُو عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بَقِيُّ بْنُ مَخْلَدٍ قَالَ: ثنا أَبُو بَكْرٍ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي شَيْبَةَ قَالَ: ثنا زِيَادُ بْنُ الرَّبِيعِ الْيَحْمَدِيُّ، عَنْ صَالِحٍ الدَّهَّانِ، قَالَ: قَالَ جَابِرُ بْنُ زَيْدٍ: الصَّوْمُ وَالصَّلَاةُ لَا يُجْهِدَانِ الْمَالَ، وَالصَّدَقَةُ تُجْهِدُ الْمَالَ، وَلَا تُجْهِدُ الْبَدَنَ، وَإِنِّي لَا أَعْلَمُ شَيْئًا لِلْمَالِ وَالْبَدَنِ مِنْ هَدْيِ الرِّعَةِ يَعْنِي الْحَجَّ

english.narrator: Abu 'Abdullah ibn Yunus -> Abu 'Abd al-Rahman Baqi ibn Makhlad -> Abu Bakr 'Abdullah ibn Muhammad ibn Abi Shaybah -> Ziyad ibn al-Rabi' al-Yahmadi -> Salih al-Dahhan
english.text: Abu 'Abdullah ibn Yunus narrated to us, saying: Abu 'Abd al-Rahman Baqi ibn Makhlad narrated to us, saying: Abu Bakr 'Abdullah ibn Muhammad ibn Abi Shaybah narrated to us, saying: Ziyad ibn al-Rabi' al-Yahmadi narrated to us from Salih al-Dahhan, who said: Jabir ibn Zayd said: "Fasting and prayer do not exhaust wealth, while charity exhausts wealth but not the body. I do not know anything for wealth and the body like Hady al-Ri'ah," meaning Hajj.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ أَبِي مِسْكِينٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ قَالَ: كَانُوا يَرَوْنَ أَنِّي أَحُجُّ مِرَارًا، إِنَّ الصَّدَقَةَ أَفْضَلُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Sufyan -> Abu Miskin -> Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki' narrated to us from Sufyan, from Abu Miskin, from Ibrahim, who said: "They used to think that I perform Hajj repeatedly, but charity is better."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا وَكِيعٌ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ بْنِ عَطِيَّةَ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ الْحَجَّاجَ، عَنْ رَجُلٍ قَضَى مَنَاسِكَ الْحَجِّ، أَيَحُجُّ أَوْ يُعْتِقُ؟ قَالَ: لَا بَلْ يُعْتِقُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Al-Hakam ibn 'Atiyyah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki' narrated to us from Al-Hakam ibn 'Atiyyah, who said: I asked Al-Hajjaj about a man who had completed the rites of Hajj, whether he should perform Hajj or free a slave. He said: "No, rather he should free a slave."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا وَهْبُ بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ الْأَسَدِيُّ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ عُبَيْدٍ الطَّائِيِّ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ قَالَ: جَاءَ أَمْضَى حَرَّانَةَ فَقَالَ: إِنِّي قَدْ تَهَيَّأْتُ لِلْخُرُوجِ، وَلِي جِيرَانٌ مُحْتَاجُونَ مُتَعَفِّفُونَ، فَمَا تَرَى إِلَى جَعْلِ كِرَايَ وَجَهَازِي فِيهِمْ، أَوْ أَمْضِي لِوَجْهِي لِلْحَجِّ؟ فَقَالَ: وَاللَّهِ إِنَّ الصَّدَقَةَ يَعْظُمُ أَجْرُهَا، وَمَا تَعْدِلُ عِنْدِي مَوْقِفًا مِنَ الْمَوَاقِفِ، أَوْ شَيْئًا مِنَ الْأَشْيَاءِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Wahb ibn Isma'il al-Asadi -> Sa'id ibn 'Ubayd al-Ta'i -> Al-Sha'bi
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Wahb ibn Isma'il al-Asadi narrated to us from Sa'id ibn 'Ubayd al-Ta'i, from Al-Sha'bi, who said: Amda Harranah came and said: "I have prepared to leave, but I have needy neighbors who refrain from asking. What do you think about giving my travel fare and provisions to them, or proceeding on my way for Hajj?" He said: "By Allah, charity brings great reward, but in my view, nothing equals a standing (at 'Arafah) or any of the rites."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ حَبِيبٍ، عَنْ حُسَيْنِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ قَالَ: لَأَنْ أَقُوتَ أَهْلَ بَيْتٍ بِالْمَدِينَةِ صَاعًا كُلَّ يَوْمٍ، أَوْ كُلَّ يَوْمٍ صَاعَيْنِ شَهْرًا، أَحَبُّ إِلَيَّ مِنْ حَجَّةٍ فِي إِثْرِ حَجَّةٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Hafs -> Layth -> Habib -> Husayn ibn 'Ali
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Hafs narrated to us from Layth, from Habib, from Husayn ibn 'Ali, who said: "To provide for a household in Madinah with a sa' every day, or two sa's every day for a month, is more beloved to me than Hajj after Hajj."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنْ جُوَيْبِرٍ، عَنِ الضَّحَّاكِ قَالَ: مَا عَلَى النَّاسِ بَعْدَ الْفَرِيضَةِ أَحَبُّ إِلَيَّ مِنْ إِطْعَامِ مِسْكِينٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu Mu'awiyah -> Juwaybir -> Al-Dahhak
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abu Mu'awiyah narrated to us from Juwaybir, from Al-Dahhak, who said: "There is nothing upon people after the obligatory duties more beloved to me than feeding a needy person."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا وَكِيعٌ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، وَعَنْ عَبْدِ الْكَرِيمِ، عَنْ مُعَاذِ بْنِ سَعْدٍ، عَنْ سِنَانِ بْنِ سَلَمَةَ، أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ قَالَ: الْهَدْيُ التَّطَوُّعُ لَا يُؤْكَلُ مِنْهُ، فَإِنْ أَكَلَ غَرِمَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Ibn Abi Layla -> 'Ata' and 'Abd al-Karim -> Mu'adh ibn Sa'd -> Sinan ibn Salamah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki' narrated to us from Ibn Abi Layla, from 'Ata', and from 'Abd al-Karim, from Mu'adh ibn Sa'd, from Sinan ibn Salamah, that the Prophet ﷺ said: "Voluntary sacrificial animals should not be eaten from; if one eats, he must pay compensation."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ ذَكْوَانَ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ، وَعَبْدِ اللَّهِ، قَالَا: إِنْ أَكَلَ مِنْهُ غَرِمَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Idris -> Ibn Jurayj -> Muhammad ibn Dhakwan -> Al-Sha'bi -> 'Ali and 'Abdullah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Idris narrated to us from Ibn Jurayj, from Muhammad ibn Dhakwan, from Al-Sha'bi, from 'Ali and 'Abdullah, who both said: "If he eats from it, he must pay compensation."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، عَنْ عَلْقَمَةَ، قَالَ: "بَعَثَ مَعِي عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بِهَدْيِهِ قَالَ: وَأَمَرَنِي إِنْ نَحَرْتُهُ: أَنْ أَتَصَدَّقَ بِثُلُثِهِ، وَآكُلَ ثُلُثًا، وَأَبْعَثَ إِلَى أَهْلِ أَخِيهِ بِثُلُثٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu Mu'awiyah -> Al-A'mash -> Ibrahim -> 'Alqamah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abu Mu'awiyah narrated to us from Al-A'mash, from Ibrahim, from 'Alqamah, who said: "'Abdullah sent his sacrificial animal with me and ordered me, if I slaughtered it, to give one-third in charity, eat one-third, and send one-third to his brother's family."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ، فِي الْبَدَنَةِ لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ شَيْءٌ فِي التَّطَوُّعِ إِلَّا أَنْ يَأْمُرَ فِيهَا بِأَمْرٍ، أَوْ يَأْكُلَ أَوْ يُطْعِمَ، فَإِنْ فَعَلَ أَبْدَلَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Abd al-A'la -> Ma'mar -> Al-Zuhri -> Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: 'Abd al-A'la narrated to us from Ma'mar, from Al-Zuhri, from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib, regarding the sacrificial camel: "There is nothing upon him regarding voluntary sacrifice unless he gives an order concerning it, eats, or feeds others. If he does so, he must replace it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنْ عَمْرٍو، عَنْ جَابِرِ بْنِ زَيْدٍ قَالَ: إِذَا أَكَلْتَ مِنْ هَدْيِ التَّطَوُّعِ غَرِمْتَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn 'Uyaynah -> 'Amr -> Jabir ibn Zayd
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn 'Uyaynah narrated to us from 'Amr, from Jabir ibn Zayd, who said: "If you eat from a voluntary sacrificial animal, you must compensate for it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ قَالَ: كَانَ مَعِي هَدْيُ صَدَقَةٍ لِلْمَسَاكِينِ، فَأَمَرَنِي أَنْ آكُلَ مِنْهُ وَأَدَّخِرَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Jarir -> Layth
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Jarir narrated to us from Layth, who said: "I had a sacrificial animal for charity for the poor, and he ordered me to eat from it and store some."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: كَانُوا لَا يَأْكُلُونَ مِنْ شَيْءٍ جَعَلُوهُ لِلَّهِ، ثُمَّ رَخَّصَ لَهُمْ أَنْ يَأْكُلُوا مِنَ الْهَدْيِ وَالْأَضَاحِيِّ وَأَشْبَاهِهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Jarir -> Mansur -> Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Jarir narrated to us from Mansur, from Ibrahim, who said: "They used not to eat anything they dedicated to Allah, then they were permitted to eat from sacrificial animals, Udhiyah, and the like."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا إِسْمَاعِيلُ ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، وَطَاوُسٍ، وَمُجَاهِدٍ، أَنَّهُمْ قَالُوا: لَا يُؤْكَلُ مِنَ الْفِدْيَةِ، وَلَا مِنْ جَزَاءِ الصَّيْدِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Isma'il ibn 'Ulayyah -> Layth -> 'Ata', Tawus, and Mujahid
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Isma'il ibn 'Ulayyah narrated to us from Layth, from 'Ata', Tawus, and Mujahid, that they said: "One should not eat from the ransom (Fidyah) nor from the penalty for hunting."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ نُمَيْرٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَقُولُ: إِذَا أُعْطِبَتِ الْبَدَنَةُ أَوْ كُسِرَتْ، أَكَلَ مِنْهَا صَاحِبُهَا، أَوْ أَطْعَمَهُ، وَلَمْ يُبَدِّلْهَا إِلَّا أَنْ يَكُونَ نَذْرًا، أَوْ جَزَاءَ صَيْدٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Numayr -> 'Abdullah -> Nafi' -> Ibn 'Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Numayr narrated to us from 'Abdullah, from Nafi', from Ibn 'Umar, that he used to say: "If the sacrificial camel becomes disabled or broken, its owner may eat from it or feed others, and he does not replace it unless it was a vow or a penalty for hunting."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ قَالَ: مَا كَانَ مِنْ جَزَاءِ صَيْدٍ، أَوْ نُسُكٍ، أَوْ نَذْرٍ لِلْمَسَاكِينِ، فَإِنَّهُ لَا يَأْكُلُ مِنْهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Idris -> 'Abd al-Malik -> 'Ata'
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Idris narrated to us from 'Abd al-Malik, from 'Ata', who said: "Whatever is a penalty for hunting, a ritual sacrifice, or a vow for the poor, one should not eat from it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ قَالَ: لَا يَأْكُلُ مِنْ جَزَاءِ الصَّيْدِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Shu'bah -> Al-Hakam -> Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki' narrated to us from Shu'bah, from Al-Hakam, from Ibrahim, who said: "He should not eat from the penalty for hunting."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ سَالِمٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ قَالَ: لَا يُؤْكَلُ مِنَ النَّذْرِ، وَلَا مِنَ الْكَفَّارَةِ، وَلَا مِمَّا جُعِلَ لِلْمَسَاكِينِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Sharik -> Salim -> Sa'id ibn Jubayr
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Sharik narrated to us from Salim, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, who said: "One should not eat from a vow, nor from an expiation, nor from what is designated for the poor."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، قَالَ: قَالَ عَلِيٌّ: لَا يُؤْكَلُ مِنَ النَّذْرِ، وَلَا مِنْ جَزَاءِ الصَّيْدِ، وَلَا مِمَّا جُعِلَ لِلْمَسَاكِينِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Sharik -> Ash'ath -> Al-Hakam -> 'Ali
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Sharik narrated to us from Ash'ath, from Al-Hakam, who said: 'Ali said: "One should not eat from a vow, nor from a penalty for hunting, nor from what is designated for the poor."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ آدَمَ، قَالَ: ثنا حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ قَيْسِ بْنِ سَعْدٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: لَا تَأْكُلْ مِنْ جَزَاءِ الصَّيْدِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Yahya ibn Adam -> Hammad ibn Salamah -> Qays ibn Sa'd -> 'Ata' -> Ibn 'Abbas
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Yahya ibn Adam narrated to us, saying: Hammad ibn Salamah narrated to us from Qays ibn Sa'd, from 'Ata', from Ibn 'Abbas, who said: "Do not eat from the penalty for hunting."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ الدَّسْتُوَائِيِّ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي حَسَّانَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ أَشْعَرَ الْهَدْيَ فِي السَّنَامِ الْأَيْمَنِ، وَأَمَاطَ عَنْهُ الدَّمَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Hisham al-Dastuwa'i -> Qatadah -> Abu Hassan -> Ibn 'Abbas
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki' narrated to us from Hisham al-Dastuwa'i, from Qatadah, from Abu Hassan, from Ibn 'Abbas, "that the Prophet ﷺ marked the sacrificial animal on the right side of the hump and wiped off the blood."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، عَنْ عُرْوَةَ، عَنِ الْمِسْوَرِ بْنِ مَخْرَمَةَ، وَمَرْوَانَ بْنِ الْحَكَمِ، أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ عَامَ الْحُدَيْبِيَةِ قَلَّدَ الْهَدْيَ وَأَشْعَرَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn 'Uyaynah -> Al-Zuhri -> 'Urwah -> Al-Miswar ibn Makhramah and Marwan ibn al-Hakam
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn 'Uyaynah narrated to us from Al-Zuhri, from 'Urwah, from Al-Miswar ibn Makhramah and Marwan ibn al-Hakam, "that the Prophet ﷺ garlanded and marked the sacrificial animal in the year of Al-Hudaybiyah."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ عَيَّاشٍ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، وَطَاوُسٍ، وَمُجَاهِدٍ، قَالُوا: لَيْسَ الْإِشْعَارُ بِوَاجِبٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu Bakr ibn 'Ayyash -> Layth -> 'Ata', Tawus, and Mujahid
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abu Bakr ibn 'Ayyash narrated to us from Layth, from 'Ata', Tawus, and Mujahid, who said: "Marking (Ish'ar) is not obligatory."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو الْأَحْوَصِ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، وَطَاوُسٍ، وَمُجَاهِدٍ قَالُوا: أَشْعِرِ الْهَدْيَ إِنْ شِئْتَ، وَإِنْ شِئْتَ فَلَا تُشْعِرْ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu al-Ahwas -> Layth -> 'Ata' and Tawus and Mujahid
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abu al-Ahwas narrated to us from Layth, from 'Ata', Tawus, and Mujahid, who said: "Mark the sacrificial animal if you wish, and if you wish, do not mark it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، عَنِ الْأَسْوَدِ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، أَنَّهُ أَرْسَلَ إِلَيْهَا تُشْعَرُ، يَعْنِي الْبَدَنَةَ، فَقَالَتْ: إِنْ شِئْتَ، إِنَّمَا تُشْعَرُ لِتُعْلَمَ أَنَّهَا بَدَنَةٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu Mu'awiyah -> Al-A'mash -> Ibrahim -> Al-Aswad -> 'Aishah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abu Mu'awiyah narrated to us from Al-A'mash, from Ibrahim, from Al-Aswad, from 'Aishah, that he sent to her asking whether the sacrificial camel should be marked. She said: "If you wish. It is only marked so that it is known to be a sacrificial animal."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ مُسْهِرٍ، عَنْ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ قَالَ: لَا هَدْيَ إِلَّا مَا قُلِّدَ، وَأُشْعِرَ، وَوَقَفَ بِعَرَفَةَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Ali ibn Mushir -> 'Ubayd Allah -> Nafi' -> Ibn 'Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: 'Ali ibn Mushir narrated to us from 'Ubayd Allah, from Nafi', from Ibn 'Umar, who said: "There is no Hady (sacrificial animal) except what has been garlanded, marked, and stood at 'Arafah."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، وَعَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ الْأَسْوَدِ أَنَّهُمَا قَالَا: تُحَلَّلُ ثُمَّ تُشْعَرُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Hafs -> Hajjaj -> 'Ata' and 'Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Aswad
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Hafs narrated to us from Hajjaj, from 'Ata' and 'Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Aswad, that they both said: "It is made lawful (Ihram is removed) and then marked."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَمَّادُ بْنُ خَالِدٍ، عَنْ أَفْلَحَ، عَنِ الْقَاسِمِ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ أَشْعَرَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Hammad ibn Khalid -> Aflah -> Al-Qasim -> 'Aishah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Hammad ibn Khalid narrated to us from Aflah, from Al-Qasim, from 'Aishah, "that the Prophet ﷺ marked (the sacrificial animal)."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ أَبِي بِشْرٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ قَالَ: الْإِبِلُ تُقَلَّدُ وَتُشْعَرُ، وَالْبَقَرُ تُقَلَّدُ وَلَا تُشْعَرُ، وَالْغَنَمُ لَا تُقَلَّدُ وَلَا تُشْعَرُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Hushaym -> Abu Bishr -> Sa'id ibn Jubayr
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Hushaym narrated to us from Abu Bishr, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, who said: "Camels are garlanded and marked, cows are garlanded but not marked, and sheep are neither garlanded nor marked."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا زَيْدُ بْنُ الْحُبَابِ، عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ قَيْسِ بْنِ سَعْدٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: إِنْ شِئْتَ فَأَشْعِرْ الْهَدْيَ، وَإِنْ شِئْتَ فَلَا تُشْعِرْ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Zayd ibn al-Hubab -> Hammad ibn Salamah -> Qays ibn Sa'd -> 'Ata' -> Ibn 'Abbas
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Zayd ibn al-Hubab narrated to us from Hammad ibn Salamah, from Qays ibn Sa'd, from 'Ata', from Ibn 'Abbas, who said: "If you wish, mark the sacrificial animal, and if you wish, do not mark it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ أَبِي بِشْرٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، وَيُوسُفَ بْنِ مَاهِكٍ، وَمَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، أَنَّ رَجُلًا أَغْلَقَ بَابَهُ عَلَى حَمَامَةٍ، وَفَرْخَيْهَا، ثُمَّ انْطَلَقَ إِلَى عَرَفَاتٍ وَمِنًى، فَرَجَعَ وَقَدْ مُوِّتَتْ، فَأَتَى ابْنَ عُمَرَ، فَذَكَرَ ذَلِكَ لَهُ، فَجَعَلَ عَلَيْهِ ثَلَاثًا مِنَ الْغَنَمِ، وَحَكَمَ مَعَهُ رَجُلٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Hushaym -> Abu Bishr -> 'Ata' and Yusuf ibn Mahik and Mansur -> 'Ata'
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Hushaym narrated to us from Abu Bishr, from 'Ata' and Yusuf ibn Mahik and Mansur, from 'Ata', that a man closed his door on a pigeon and its two chicks, then went to 'Arafat and Mina. He returned and they had died. He came to Ibn 'Umar and mentioned that to him, so he imposed upon him three sheep, and another man judged with him.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءِ بْنِ السَّائِبِ قَالَ: نَزَلْنَا مَنْزِلًا، فَأَغْلَقْتُ بَابَ الْمَنْزِلِ عَلَى حَمَامَةٍ، فَمَاتَتْ، فَسَأَلْتُ عَطَاءً فَقَالَ: فِيهَا شَاةٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Fudayl -> 'Ata' ibn al-Sa'ib
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Fudayl narrated to us from 'Ata' ibn al-Sa'ib, who said: We stayed at a lodging, and I closed the door of the lodging on a pigeon, and it died. I asked 'Ata', and he said: "There is a sheep for it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو خَالِدٍ الْأَحْمَرُ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ قَالَ: عَلَيْهِ شَاةٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu Khalid al-Ahmar -> Yahya ibn Sa'id -> Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abu Khalid al-Ahmar narrated to us from Yahya ibn Sa'id, from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib, who said: "He must offer a sheep."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو خَالِدٍ الْأَحْمَرُ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ قَالَ: مَنْ قَتَلَ حَمَامَةً مِنْ حَمَامِ مَكَّةَ فَعَلَيْهِ شَاةٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu Khalid al-Ahmar -> Ash'ath -> 'Ata'
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abu Khalid al-Ahmar narrated to us from Ash'ath, from 'Ata', who said: "Whoever kills a pigeon from the pigeons of Makkah must offer a sheep."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو خَالِدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ قَالَ: عَلَيْهِ شَاةٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu Khalid -> Ibn Jurayj -> 'Ata'
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abu Khalid narrated to us from Ibn Jurayj, from 'Ata', who said: "He must offer a sheep."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سَلَمَةَ بْنِ مُحْرِزٍ قَالَ: أَغْلَقْتُ بَابِي بِمَكَّةَ ثُمَّ فَتَحْتُهُ، فَإِذَا طَيْرَيْنِ قَدْ مَاتَا، فَسَأَلْتُ طَاوُسًا فَقَالَ: اذْبَحْ شَاتَيْنِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Salamah ibn Muhriz
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki' narrated to us from Salamah ibn Muhriz, who said: I closed my door in Makkah, then opened it and found two birds dead. I asked Tawus, and he said: "Slaughter two sheep."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، فِي طَيْرِ الْحَرَمِ شَاةٌ شَاةٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Ibn Abi Layla -> 'Ata' -> Ibn 'Abbas
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki' narrated to us from Ibn Abi Layla, from 'Ata', from Ibn 'Abbas: "For the birds of the Haram, (the penalty is) a sheep for each."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، فِي الْبُرْسِيِّ وَالْقُمْرِيِّ وَالْأَخْفَرِ شَاةٌ شَاةٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Ibn Abi Layla -> 'Ata'
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki' narrated to us from Ibn Abi Layla, from 'Ata': "For the Bursiy (sparrow?), the Qumri (dove), and the Akhfar (bird?), (the penalty is) a sheep for each."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، عَنْ شَيْخٍ مِنْ أَهْلِ مَكَّةَ، أَنَّ حَمَامًا كَانَ عَلَى الْبَيْتِ، فَخَرَّتْ عَلَى يَدِ عُمَرُ، فَأَشَارَ بِيَدِهِ فَطَارَ، فَوَقَعَ عَلَى مُقْصِرِ بُيُوتِ أَهْلِ مَكَّةَ، فَجَاءَتْ حَيَّةٌ فَأَكَلَتْهُ، فَحَكَمَ عُمَرُ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ شَاةً

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ghundar -> Shu'bah -> Al-Hakam -> A sheikh from the people of Makkah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ghundar narrated to us from Shu'bah, from Al-Hakam, from a sheikh from the people of Makkah, that a pigeon was on the House, and it fell onto 'Umar's hand. He gestured with his hand, so it flew and landed on one of the houses of the people of Makkah. Then a snake came and ate it, so 'Umar judged upon himself (to sacrifice) a sheep.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ الْقَطَّانُ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي يَحْيَى، عَنْ صَالِحِ بْنِ الْمَهْدِيِّ، أَنَّ أَبَاهُ، أَخْبَرَهُ قَالَ: حَجَجْتُ مَعَ عُثْمَانَ، فَقَدِمْنَا بِمَكَّةَ، فَفَرَشْتُ لَهُ فِي بَيْتٍ، فَرَقَدَ فَجَاءَتْ حَمَامَةٌ، فَوَقَعَتْ فِي كُوَّةٍ عَلَى فِرَاشِهِ، فَجَعَلَتْ تَبْحَثُ بِرِجْلَيْهَا، فَخَشِيتُ أَنْ تَنْثُرَ عَلَى فِرَاشِهِ، فَيَسْتَيْقِظَ فَأَطَرْتُهَا، فَوَقَعَتْ فِي كُوَّةٍ أُخْرَى، فَخَرَجَتْ حَيَّةٌ، فَقَتَلَتْهَا، فَلَمَّا اسْتَيْقَظَ عُثْمَانُ أَخْبَرْتُهُ، فَقَالَ: أَدِّ عَنْكَ شَاةً فَقَالَ: إِنَّمَا أَطَرْتُهَا مِنْ أَجْلِكَ، قَالَ: وَعَنِّي شَاةً

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Yahya ibn Sa'id al-Qattan -> Muhammad ibn Abi Yahya -> Salih ibn al-Mahdi -> His Father
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Yahya ibn Sa'id al-Qattan narrated to us from Muhammad ibn Abi Yahya, from Salih ibn al-Mahdi, that his father informed him, saying: I performed Hajj with 'Uthman. We arrived in Makkah, and I spread bedding for him in a house. He slept, and a pigeon came and landed in a window above his bedding. It started scratching with its feet, so I feared it would scatter dust on his bedding and wake him up. I shooed it away, and it landed in another window where a snake came out and killed it. When 'Uthman woke up, I told him, and he said: "Pay a sheep for yourself." I said: "I only shooed it away for your sake." He said: "And a sheep for me."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ إِسْرَائِيلَ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ قَالَ: أَوَّلُ مَنْ فَدَى طَيْرَ الْحَرَمِ شَاةً عُثْمَانُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Isra'il -> Jabir -> 'Ata'
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki' narrated to us from Isra'il, from Jabir, from 'Ata', who said: "The first person to redeem a bird of the Haram with a sheep was 'Uthman."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدَةُ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَقُولُ فِي حَمَامِ الْحَرَمِ: إِذَا قُتِلَ بِمَكَّةَ فَفِيهِ شَاةٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Abdah -> Yahya ibn Sa'id -> Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: 'Abdah narrated to us from Yahya ibn Sa'id, from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib, that he used to say regarding the pigeons of the Haram: "If one is killed in Makkah, there is a sheep for it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، وَحَمَّادٍ، قَالَا: سَأَلْنَا إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ رَجُلٍ أَخَذَ بِيَدِهِ فَرْخًا، قَالَ: أَرَادَ أَنْ يَرُدَّهُ فَمَاتَ، فَقَالَ: هُوَ ضَامِنٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Hafs -> Ash'ath -> Al-Hakam and Hammad
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Hafs narrated to us from Ash'ath, from Al-Hakam and Hammad, who said: We asked Ibrahim about a man who took a chick in his hand, intending to return it, but it died. He said: "He is liable."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ بْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنْ خُصَيْفٍ، عَنْ مِقْسَمٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: {لَا رَفَثَ} : الْجِمَاعُ، {وَلَا فُسُوقَ} [البقرة: 197] : الْمَعَاصِي، {وَلَا جِدَالَ فِي الْحَجِّ} [البقرة: 197] قَالَ: تُمَارِي صَاحِبَكَ حَتَّى تُغْضِبَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Sufyan ibn 'Uyaynah -> Khusayf -> Miqsam -> Ibn 'Abbas
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Sufyan ibn 'Uyaynah narrated to us from Khusayf, from Miqsam, from Ibn 'Abbas, who said: "{There is to be no lewdness (Rafath)} means sexual intercourse, {and no disobedience (Fusuq)} [Al-Baqarah: 197] means sins, {and no disputing (Jidal) during Hajj} [Al-Baqarah: 197] means arguing with your companion until you anger him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي نَجِيحٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، {وَلَا جِدَالَ فِي الْحَجِّ} [البقرة: 197] قَالَ: قَدْ صَارَ الْحَجُّ فِي ذِي الْحِجَّةِ، فَلَا شَهْرَ يُنْسَأُ، وَلَا شَكَّ فِي الْحَجِّ، لِأَنَّ أَهْلَ الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ كَانُوا يُسْقِطُونَ الْمُحَرَّمَ، فَيَحُجُّونَ فِي غَيْرِ ذِي الْحِجَّةِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn 'Uyaynah -> Ibn Abi Najih -> Mujahid
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn 'Uyaynah narrated to us from Ibn Abi Najih, from Mujahid, regarding {And no disputing during Hajj} [Al-Baqarah: 197]. He said: "Hajj has been fixed in Dhu al-Hijjah, so no month is postponed, and there is no doubt in Hajj, because the people of Jahiliyyah used to drop Muharram and perform Hajj in a month other than Dhu al-Hijjah."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ بْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنْ عَمْرٍو، عَنْ جَابِرِ بْنِ زَيْدٍ قَالَ: {لَا جِدَالَ فِي الْحَجِّ} [البقرة: 197] قَالَ: لَيْسَ لَكَ أَنْ تُمَارِيَ صَاحِبَكَ حَتَّى تُغْضِبَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Sufyan ibn 'Uyaynah -> 'Amr -> Jabir ibn Zayd
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Sufyan ibn 'Uyaynah narrated to us from 'Amr, from Jabir ibn Zayd, who said regarding {And no disputing during Hajj} [Al-Baqarah: 197]: "It is not for you to argue with your companion until you anger him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ قَالَ: "الرَّفَثُ: إِتْيَانُ النِّسَاءِ، وَالْفُسُوقُ: السِّبَابُ، وَالْجِدَالُ: الْمُمَارَاةُ أَنْ تُمَارِيَ صَاحِبَكَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Muhammad ibn Fudayl -> Mughirah -> Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Muhammad ibn Fudayl narrated to us from Mughirah, from Ibrahim, who said: "Rafath is having intercourse with women, Fusuq is reviling, and Jidal is arguing with your companion."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ حُسَيْنِ بْنِ عَقِيلٍ، عَنِ الضَّحَّاكِ قَالَ: "الرَّفَثُ: الْجِمَاعُ، وَالْفُسُوقُ: الْمَعَاصِي، وَالْجِدَالُ: الْمِرَاءُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Husayn ibn 'Aqil -> Al-Dahhak
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki' narrated to us from Husayn ibn 'Aqil, from Al-Dahhak, who said: "Rafath is intercourse, Fusuq is disobedience, and Jidal is arguing."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ عَاصِمٍ، عَنْ بَكْرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، قَالَ: "الرَّفَثُ: الْجِمَاعُ، وَلَكِنَّ اللَّهَ كَنَّى

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Sufyan -> 'Asim -> Bakr -> Ibn 'Abbas
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki' narrated to us from Sufyan, from 'Asim, from Bakr, from Ibn 'Abbas, who said: "Rafath is intercourse, but Allah used a metonymy."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ يُونُسَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ: "الرَّفَثُ: الْغِشْيَانُ، وَالْفُسُوقُ: السِّبَابُ، وَالْجِدَالُ: الِاخْتِلَافُ فِي الْحَجِّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Abd al-A'la -> Yunus -> Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: 'Abd al-A'la narrated to us from Yunus, from Al-Hasan, who said: "Rafath is intercourse, Fusuq is reviling, and Jidal is disagreement regarding Hajj."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ آدَمَ، عَنْ وَهْبٍ، عَنْ مُوسَى بْنِ عُقْبَةَ، أَنَّهُ سَأَلَ عَطَاءَ بْنَ يَسَارٍ عَنْ قَوْلِهِ {فَلَا رَفَثَ وَلَا فُسُوقَ وَلَا جِدَالَ فِي الْحَجِّ} [البقرة: 197] قَالَ: "الرَّفَثُ: وِقَاعُ النِّسَاءِ، وَالْفُسُوقُ: الْمَعَاصِي، وَالْجِدَالُ: السِّبَابُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Yahya ibn Adam -> Wahb -> Musa ibn 'Uqbah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Yahya ibn Adam narrated to us from Wahb, from Musa ibn 'Uqbah, that he asked 'Ata' ibn Yasar about the saying: {There is to be no lewdness nor disobedience nor disputing during Hajj} [Al-Baqarah: 197]. He said: "Rafath is intercourse with women, Fusuq is disobedience, and Jidal is reviling."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ نُمَيْرٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ قَالَ: "الرَّفَثُ وَالْفُسُوقُ: الْمَعَاصِي، وَالْجِدَالُ: أَنْ تُجَادِلَ صَاحِبَكَ حَتَّى تُغْضِبَهُ وَيُغْضِبَكَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Abdullah ibn Numayr -> 'Abd al-Malik -> 'Ata'
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: 'Abdullah ibn Numayr narrated to us from 'Abd al-Malik, from 'Ata', who said: "Rafath and Fusuq are disobedience, and Jidal is to argue with your companion until you anger him and he angers you."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ بْنِ رُفَيْعٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، {وَلَا جِدَالَ فِي الْحَجِّ} [البقرة: 197] قَالَ: قَدِ اسْتَقَامَ أَمْرُ الْحَجِّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Mahdi -> Sufyan -> 'Abd al-'Aziz ibn Rufay' -> Mujahid
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Mahdi narrated to us from Sufyan, from 'Abd al-'Aziz ibn Rufay', from Mujahid, regarding {And no disputing during Hajj} [Al-Baqarah: 197]. He said: "The matter of Hajj has been established."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ أَبِي خَالِدٍ الْوَالِبِيِّ، عَنِ النُّعْمَانِ بْنِ عَمْرِو بْنِ مُقَرِّنٍ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: سِبَابُ الْمُؤْمِنِ فُسُوقٌ، وَقِتَالُهُ كُفْرٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Jarir -> Mansur -> Abu Khalid al-Walibi -> Al-Nu'man ibn 'Amr ibn Muqarrin
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Jarir narrated to us from Mansur, from Abu Khalid al-Walibi, from Al-Nu'man ibn 'Amr ibn Muqarrin, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Reviling a believer is Fusuq (disobedience), and fighting him is Kufr (disbelief)."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدَةُ، عَنْ حُمَيْدٍ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ أَبِي خَالِدٍ الْوَالِبِيِّ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ النُّعْمَانِ بْنِ مُقَرِّنٍ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ بِنَحْوِهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Abdah -> Humayd -> Mansur -> Abu Khalid al-Walibi -> 'Amr ibn al-Nu'man ibn Muqarrin
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: 'Abdah narrated to us from Humayd, from Mansur, from Abu Khalid al-Walibi, from 'Amr ibn al-Nu'man ibn Muqarrin, from the Prophet ﷺ, similarly.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنْ شَرِيكٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ مُهَاجِرٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ قَالَ: "الرَّفَثُ: الْجِمَاعُ، وَالْفُسُوقُ: السِّبَابُ، وَالْجِدَالُ: الْمِرَاءُ، أَنْ تُمَارِيَ صَاحِبَكَ حَتَّى تُغْضِبَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu Usamah -> Sharik -> Ibrahim ibn Muhajir -> Mujahid -> Ibn 'Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abu Usamah narrated to us from Sharik, from Ibrahim ibn Muhajir, from Mujahid, from Ibn 'Umar, who said: "Rafath is intercourse, Fusuq is reviling, and Jidal is disputing, to argue with your companion until you anger him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ هِشَامٍ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ بْنِ رُفَيْعٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ قَالَ: "الرَّفَثُ: الْجِمَاعُ، وَالْفُسُوقُ: الْمَعَاصِي، وَالْجِدَالُ: الْمِرَاءُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Mu'awiyah ibn Hisham -> Sufyan -> 'Abd al-'Aziz ibn Rufay' -> Mujahid
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Mu'awiyah ibn Hisham narrated to us from Sufyan, from 'Abd al-'Aziz ibn Rufay', from Mujahid, who said: "Rafath is intercourse, Fusuq is disobedience, and Jidal is disputing."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شَبَابَةُ، عَنْ وَرْقَاءَ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي نَجِيحٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، {فَلَا رَفَثَ} [البقرة: 197] قَالَ: جِمَاعُ النِّسَاءِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Shababah -> Warqa' -> Ibn Abi Najih -> Mujahid
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Shababah narrated to us from Warqa', from Ibn Abi Najih, from Mujahid regarding {There is to be no lewdness} [Al-Baqarah: 197]. He said: "Intercourse with women."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُعْتَمِرُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو عَمْرٍو الشَّيْبَانِيُّ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ عَبْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ مَسْعُودٍ يَقُولُ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: سِبَابُ الْمُؤْمِنِ فُسُوقٌ، وَقِتَالُهُ كُفْرٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Mu'tamir ibn Sulayman -> His Father -> Abu 'Amr al-Shaybani -> 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Mu'tamir ibn Sulayman narrated to us from his father, saying: Abu 'Amr al-Shaybani narrated to us, saying: I heard 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud say: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Reviling a believer is Fusuq, and fighting him is Kufr."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ أَبِي وَائِلٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ بِنَحْوِ حَدِيثِ مُعْتَمِرٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ghundar -> Shu'bah -> Mansur -> Abu Wa'il -> 'Abdullah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ghundar narrated to us from Shu'bah, from Mansur, from Abu Wa'il, from 'Abdullah, from the Prophet ﷺ, similar to the Hadith of Mu'tamir.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الْأَسَدِيُّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو هِلَالٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ سِيرِينَ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: سِبَابُ الْمُؤْمِنِ فُسُوقٌ، وَقِتَالُهُ كُفْرٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Muhammad ibn al-Hasan al-Asadi -> Abu Hilal -> Ibn Sirin -> Abu Hurayrah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Muhammad ibn al-Hasan al-Asadi narrated to us, saying: Abu Hilal narrated to us from Ibn Sirin, from Abu Hurayrah, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Reviling a believer is Fusuq, and fighting him is Kufr."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ بْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي الزُّبَيْرِ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ بَابَاهُ، عَنْ جُبَيْرِ بْنِ مُطْعِمٍ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: يَا بَنِي عَبْدِ مَنَافٍ، لَا تَمْنَعُوا أَحَدًا طَافَ بِهَذَا الْبَيْتِ، وَصَلَّى أَيَّ سَاعَةٍ مِنْ لَيْلٍ أَوْ نَهَارٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Sufyan ibn 'Uyaynah -> Abu al-Zubayr -> 'Abdullah ibn Babah -> Jubayr ibn Mut'im
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Sufyan ibn 'Uyaynah narrated to us from Abu al-Zubayr, from 'Abdullah ibn Babah, from Jubayr ibn Mut'im, from the Prophet ﷺ that he said: "O Banu 'Abd Manaf, do not prevent anyone who performs Tawaf around this House and prays at any hour of the night or day."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي أَبُو الْأَحْوَصِ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ ابْنَ عُمَرَ، وَابْنَ عَبَّاسٍ طَافَا بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ، وَصَلَّيَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu al-Ahwas -> Layth -> 'Ata'
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abu al-Ahwas narrated to me from Layth, from 'Ata', who said: "I saw Ibn 'Umar and Ibn 'Abbas perform Tawaf after 'Asr and pray."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو الْأَحْوَصِ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ ابْنَ عُمَرَ طَافَ الْبَيْتَ عِنْدَ الْفَجْرِ، وَصَلَّى الرَّكْعَتَيْنِ قَبْلَ طُلُوعِ الشَّمْسِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu al-Ahwas -> Abu Ishaq -> 'Ata'
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abu al-Ahwas narrated to us from Abu Ishaq, from 'Ata', who said: "I saw Ibn 'Umar perform Tawaf around the House at dawn and pray the two Rak'ahs before the sun rose."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، أَنَّهُ رَأَى الْحَسَنَ، وَالْحُسَيْنَ قَدِمَا مَكَّةَ فَطَافَا بِالْبَيْتِ بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ وَصَلَّيَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Muhammad ibn Fudayl -> Layth -> Shu'bah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Muhammad ibn Fudayl narrated to us from Layth, from Shu'bah, "that he saw Al-Hasan and Al-Husayn arrive in Makkah, perform Tawaf around the House after 'Asr, and pray."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُعْتَمِرُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ التَّيْمِيُّ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، أَنَّ الْحَسَنَ، وَعَطَاءً، وَمُجَاهِدًا، كَانُوا يَطُوفُونَ بِالْبَيْتِ بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ، وَيُصَلُّونَ فِي دُبُرِ صَلَاتِهِمْ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Mu'tamir ibn Sulayman al-Taymi -> Layth
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Mu'tamir ibn Sulayman al-Taymi narrated to us from Layth, "that Al-Hasan, 'Ata', and Mujahid used to perform Tawaf around the House after 'Asr and pray after their prayer."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو دَاوُدَ الطَّيَالِسِيُّ، عَنْ سُلَيْمِ بْنِ حَيَّانَ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ عِكْرِمَةَ بْنَ خَالِدٍ عَنْهُ، فَقَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu Dawud al-Tayalisi -> Sulaym ibn Hayyan
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abu Dawud al-Tayalisi narrated to us from Sulaym ibn Hayyan, who said: I asked 'Ikrimah ibn Khalid about it, and he said: "There is no harm."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو دَاوُدَ، عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، أَنَّهُ لَمْ يَرَ بَأْسًا بِالطَّوَافِ بَعْدَ الْفَجْرِ، وَبَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ وَالصَّلَاةِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu Dawud -> Hammad ibn Salamah -> Hisham -> His Father
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abu Dawud narrated to us from Hammad ibn Salamah, from Hisham, from his father, "that he saw no harm in Tawaf after dawn and after 'Asr, and praying."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنِ الْوَلِيدِ بْنِ جُمَيْعٍ، عَنْ أَبِي الطُّفَيْلِ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَطُوفُ بِالْبَيْتِ بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ، وَيُصَلِّي حِينَ تَصْفَرُّ الشَّمْسُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Muhammad ibn Fudayl -> Al-Walid ibn Jumay' -> Abu al-Tufayl
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Muhammad ibn Fudayl narrated to us from Al-Walid ibn Jumay', from Abu al-Tufayl, "that he used to perform Tawaf around the House after 'Asr and pray when the sun turned yellow."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَعْلَى بْنُ عُبَيْدٍ، عَنِ الْأَجْلَحِ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ ابْنَ عُمَرَ، وَابْنَ الزُّبَيْرِ، طَافَا بِالْبَيْتِ بَعْدَ صَلَاةِ الْفَجْرِ، ثُمَّ صَلَّيَا رَكْعَتَيْنِ قَبْلَ طُلُوعِ الشَّمْسِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ya'la ibn 'Ubayd -> Al-Ajlah -> 'Ata'
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ya'la ibn 'Ubayd narrated to us from Al-Ajlah, from 'Ata', who said: "I saw Ibn 'Umar and Ibn al-Zubayr perform Tawaf around the House after the Dawn prayer, then they prayed two Rak'ahs before the sun rose."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ مِسْعَرٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ بْنِ مَيْسَرَةَ، عَنْ طَاوُسٍ قَالَ: طُفْ وَصَلِّ بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ، وَبَعْدَ الْفَجْرِ، مَا كُنْتَ فِي وَقْتٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Mis'ar -> 'Abd al-Malik ibn Maysarah -> Tawus
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki' narrated to us from Mis'ar, from 'Abd al-Malik ibn Maysarah, from Tawus, who said: "Perform Tawaf and pray after 'Asr and after Dawn, as long as you are within the time."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ نُمَيْرٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُرْوَةَ بْنِ الزُّبَيْرِ، أَنَّ ثَابِتَ بْنَ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الزُّبَيْرِ طَافَ سَبْعًا بَعْدَ صَلَاةِ الصُّبْحِ، فَجَلَسَ وَلَمْ يُصَلِّ، فَجَاءَهُ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ الزُّبَيْرِ فَقَالَ: يَا بُنَيَّ، إِنْ كُنْتَ طَائِفًا فَصَلِّ، وَإِذَا لَمْ تُصَلِّ فَلَا تَطُفْ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Abdullah ibn Numayr -> 'Abdullah ibn Muslim -> 'Amr ibn 'Abdullah ibn 'Urwah ibn al-Zubayr
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: 'Abdullah ibn Numayr narrated to us from 'Abdullah ibn Muslim, from 'Amr ibn 'Abdullah ibn 'Urwah ibn al-Zubayr, that Thabit ibn 'Abdullah ibn al-Zubayr performed Tawaf seven times after the Morning prayer, then sat down and did not pray. 'Abdullah ibn al-Zubayr came to him and said: "O my son, if you perform Tawaf, then pray, and if you do not pray, then do not perform Tawaf."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا الْفَضْلُ بْنُ دُكَيْنٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ طَهْمَانَ، عَنْ أَبِي الزُّبَيْرِ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ بَابَاهُ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ أَبَا الدَّرْدَاءِ طَافَ بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ، وَصَلَّى رَكْعَتَيْنِ، فَقِيلَ لَهُ، فَقَالَ: إِنَّهَا لَيْسَتْ كَسَائِرِهَا مِنَ الْبُلْدَانِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Al-Fadl ibn Dukayn -> Ibrahim ibn Tahman -> Abu al-Zubayr -> 'Abdullah ibn Babah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Al-Fadl ibn Dukayn narrated to us from Ibrahim ibn Tahman, from Abu al-Zubayr, from 'Abdullah ibn Babah, who said: I saw Abu al-Darda' perform Tawaf after 'Asr and pray two Rak'ahs. It was said to him (about it), and he replied: "It (Makkah) is not like other lands."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى بْنُ عَبْدِ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ قَالَ: كَانَ الْمِسْوَرُ بْنُ مَخْرَمَةَ يَطُوفُ بِالْغَدَاةِ بِثَلَاثَةِ أَسَابِيعَ، فَإِذَا طَلَعَتِ الشَّمْسُ، صَلَّى لِكُلِّ أُسْبُوعٍ رَكْعَتَيْنِ، وَبَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ يَفْعَلُ ذَلِكَ، فَإِذَا غَابَتِ الشَّمْسُ، صَلَّى لِكُلِّ أُسْبُوعٍ رَكْعَتَيْنِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Abd al-A'la ibn 'Abd al-A'la -> Hisham -> 'Ata'
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: 'Abd al-A'la ibn 'Abd al-A'la narrated to us from Hisham, from 'Ata', who said: "Al-Miswar ibn Makhramah used to perform Tawaf in the morning for three weeks (circuits). When the sun rose, he prayed two Rak'ahs for each week. He did the same after 'Asr; when the sun set, he prayed two Rak'ahs for each week."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْوَهَّابِ الثَّقَفِيُّ، عَنْ أَيُّوبَ قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ سَعِيدَ بْنَ جُبَيْرٍ، وَمُجَاهِدًا يَطُوفَانِ بِالْبَيْتِ حَتَّى تَصْفَارَّ الشَّمْسُ وَيَجْلِسَانِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Abd al-Wahhab al-Thaqafi -> Ayyub
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: 'Abd al-Wahhab al-Thaqafi narrated to us from Ayyub, who said: "I saw Sa'id ibn Jubayr and Mujahid performing Tawaf around the House until the sun turned yellow, and they were sitting."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، أَنَّهَا قَالَتْ: إِذَا أَرَدْتَ الطَّوَافَ بِالْبَيْتِ بَعْدَ صَلَاةِ الْفَجْرِ، أَوْ بَعْدَ صَلَاةِ الْعَصْرِ، فَطُفْ، وَأَخِّرِ الصَّلَاةَ حَتَّى تَغِيبَ الشَّمْسُ، وَحَتَّى تَطْلُعَ، فَصَلِّ لِكُلِّ أُسْبُوعٍ رَكْعَتَيْنِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Muhammad ibn Fudayl -> 'Abd al-Malik -> 'Ata' -> 'Aishah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Muhammad ibn Fudayl narrated to us from 'Abd al-Malik, from 'Ata', from 'Aishah, that she said: "If you want to perform Tawaf around the House after the Dawn prayer or after the 'Asr prayer, then perform Tawaf, but delay the prayer until the sun sets or until it rises. Then pray two Rak'ahs for every week (seven circuits)."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، عَنْ نَضْرِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، عَنْ جَدِّهِ مُعَاذٍ الْقُرَشِيِّ، أَنَّهُ طَافَ بِالْبَيْتِ مَعَ مُعَاذِ ابْنِ عَفْرَاءَ بَعْدَ الْفَجْرِ، وَبَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ، فَلَمْ يُصَلِّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ghundar -> Shu'bah -> Sa'id ibn Ibrahim -> Nadr ibn 'Abd al-Rahman -> His Grandfather Mu'adh al-Qurashi
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ghundar narrated to us from Shu'bah, from Sa'id ibn Ibrahim, from Nadr ibn 'Abd al-Rahman, from his grandfather Mu'adh al-Qurashi, "that he performed Tawaf around the House with Mu'adh ibn 'Afra' after Dawn and after 'Asr, and he did not pray."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ مُسْهِرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ قَالَ: طَافَ عُمَرُ بْنُ الْخَطَّابِ بَعْدَ الْفَجْرِ، ثُمَّ رَكِبَ، حَتَّى إِذَا أَتَى ذَاتَ طُوًى نَزَلَ، فَلَمَّا طَلَعَتِ الشَّمْسُ وَارْتَفَعَتْ، صَلَّى رَكْعَتَيْنِ، ثُمَّ قَالَ: رَكْعَتَيْنِ مَكَانَ رَكْعَتَيْنِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Ali ibn Mushir -> Ibn Abi Layla -> 'Ata'
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: 'Ali ibn Mushir narrated to us from Ibn Abi Layla, from 'Ata', who said: 'Umar ibn al-Khattab performed Tawaf after Dawn, then rode until he reached Dhat Tuwa, where he dismounted. When the sun rose and ascended, he prayed two Rak'ahs, then said: "Two Rak'ahs in place of two Rak'ahs."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو دَاوُدَ الطَّيَالِسِيُّ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ الدَّسْتُوَائِيِّ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي نَجِيحٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ قَالَ: صَلَّيْتُ الصُّبْحَ، ثُمَّ جَلَسْنَا نَنْتَظِرُ بِالطَّوَافِ قَالَ: فَطَافَ أَبُو سَعِيدٍ الْخُدْرِيِّ ثُمَّ جَلَسَ وَلَمْ يُصَلِّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu Dawud al-Tayalisi -> Hisham al-Dastuwa'i -> Ibn Abi Najih -> His Father
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abu Dawud al-Tayalisi narrated to us from Hisham al-Dastuwa'i, from Ibn Abi Najih, from his father, who said: I prayed the Morning prayer, then we sat waiting by the Tawaf area. He said: "Abu Sa'id al-Khudri performed Tawaf, then sat down and did not pray."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْحَمِيدِ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ قَالَ: رُبَّمَا أَخَذْتُ النَّمْلَةَ بِعَرَفَةَ، قَدْ عَضَّتْ بَطْنِي، فَأَقْطَعُ رَأْسَهَا، وَيَبْقَى سَائِرُهَا فِي بَطْنِي

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Jarir -> 'Abd al-Hamid -> Layth -> Mujahid
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Jarir narrated to us from 'Abd al-Hamid, from Layth, from Mujahid, who said: "Sometimes I would catch an ant at 'Arafah that had bitten my stomach, so I would cut off its head, and the rest of it would remain on my stomach."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ، قَالَ: سُئِلَ عَطَاءٌ عَنْ رَجُلٍ قَتَلَ ذَرًّا كَثِيرًا لَا يَدْرِي مَا يُحَدِّدُهُ، قَالَ: يَتَصَدَّقُ بِتَمْرٍ كَثِيرٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Hafs -> 'Abd al-Malik
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Hafs narrated to us from 'Abd al-Malik, who said: 'Ata' was asked about a man who killed many ants and could not determine the number. He said: "He should give a lot of dates in charity."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصُ بْنُ غِيَاثٍ، عَنِ الْعَلَاءِ بْنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ، عَنِ الْوَلِيدِ الْبَجَلِيِّ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، فِي مُحْرِمٍ أَصَابَ ذَرًّا كَثِيرًا، قَالَ: يَتَصَدَّقُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Hafs ibn Ghiyath -> Al-'Ala' ibn al-Musayyib -> Al-Walid al-Bajali -> Sa'id ibn Jubayr
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Hafs ibn Ghiyath narrated to us from Al-'Ala' ibn al-Musayyib, from Al-Walid al-Bajali, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, regarding a Muhrim who killed many ants. He said: "He gives charity."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ طَاوُسًا، وَسَأَلَهُ رَجُلٌ فَقَالَ: أَهْلَلْتُ فَقَتَلْتُ ذَرًّا كَثِيرًا، قَالَ: تَصَدَّقْ بِقَبَضَاتٍ مِنْ قَمْحٍ

english.narrator: Ibn 'Uyaynah -> Ibn Jurayj
english.text: Ibn 'Uyaynah narrated to us from Ibn Jurayj, who said: I heard Tawus being asked by a man who said: "I assumed Ihram and killed many ants." He said: "Give handfuls of wheat in charity."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، فِي النَّمْلِ يَقْتُلُهُ الْمُحْرِمُ، قَالَ: يُطْعِمُ شَيْئًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Ibn Abi Layla -> 'Ata'
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki' narrated to us from Ibn Abi Layla, from 'Ata', regarding ants killed by a Muhrim. He said: "He feeds something."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ رَافِعٍ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ طَاوُسًا، عَنْ قَتْلِ الذَّرِّ فِي الْحَرَمِ، فَقَالَ: إِذَا آذَاكَ فَلَا بَأْسَ بِهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Ibrahim ibn Rafi'
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki' narrated to us from Ibrahim ibn Rafi', who said: I asked Tawus about killing ants in the Haram. He said: "If they harm you, there is no harm in it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ الْقَاسِمَ، وَمُجَاهِدًا، وَسَالِمًا، وَعَطَاءً، وَطَاوُسًا، عَنِ النَّمْلِ وَالْجَنَادِبِ وَالْقَطَا فَقَالُوا: إِنْ كَانَ خَطَأً فَلَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ شَيْءٌ، وَإِنْ كَانَ عَمْدًا، فَفِيهِ كَفٌّ مِنْ طَعَامٍ وَقَالَ عَامِرٌ: هُوَ كَفٌّ مِنْ طَعَامٍ خَطَأً كَانَ أَمْ عَمْدًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Sufyan -> Jabir
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki' narrated to us from Sufyan, from Jabir, who said: I asked Al-Qasim, Mujahid, Salim, 'Ata', and Tawus about ants, grasshoppers, and sandgrouse. They said: "If it was by mistake, there is nothing upon him. If it was intentional, then there is a handful of food for it." 'Amir said: "It is a handful of food whether by mistake or intentional."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَسْبَاطُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو، عَنْ أَبِي أُمَامَةَ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ ابْنَ عُمَرَ قَالَ: قُلْتُ: أَقْتُلُ الْبَعُوضَ؟ قَالَ: وَمَا عَلَيْكَ؟

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Asbat ibn Muhammad -> Al-Hasan ibn 'Amr -> Abu Umamah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Asbat ibn Muhammad narrated to us from Al-Hasan ibn 'Amr, from Abu Umamah, who said: I asked Ibn 'Umar: "Can I kill mosquitoes?" He said: "What is the harm upon you?"

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عِيسَى بْنُ يُونُسَ، عَنْ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَبِي زِيَادٍ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ سَالِمًا قَتَلَ بَعُوضَةً بِمَكَّةَ، فَقُلْتُ لَهُ، فَقَالَ: إِنَّهُ قَدْ أُمِرَ بِقَتْلِ الْحَيَّةِ وَالْعَقْرَبِ قُلْتُ: إِنَّهَا عَدُوٌّ، قَالَ: فَهَذِهِ عَدُوٌّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Isa ibn Yunus -> 'Ubayd Allah ibn Abi Ziyad
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: 'Isa ibn Yunus narrated to us from 'Ubayd Allah ibn Abi Ziyad, who said: I saw Salim kill a mosquito in Makkah. I spoke to him about it, and he said: "We have been ordered to kill the snake and the scorpion." I said: "Those are enemies." He said: "And this is an enemy."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ قَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ أَنْ تَقْتُلَ الذُّبَابَ وَالْبَعُوضَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Yahya ibn Sa'id -> Ibn Jurayj -> 'Ata'
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Yahya ibn Sa'id narrated to us from Ibn Jurayj, from 'Ata', who said: "There is no harm in killing flies and mosquitoes."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مَرْزُوقٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، فِيمَنْ قَتَلَ ذُبَابًا قَالَ: لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ شَيْءٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Jarir -> Marzuq -> Sa'id ibn Jubayr
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Jarir narrated to us from Marzuq, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, regarding someone who killed a fly. He said: "There is nothing upon him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ بْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنْ أَيُّوبَ بْنِ مُوسَى، عَنْ نُبَيْهِ بْنِ وَهْبٍ، عَنْ أَبَانَ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ، أَنَّهُ أَخْبَرَهُ، أَنَّ عُثْمَانَ حَدَّثَ، عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، فِي الرَّجُلِ إِذَا اشْتَكَى عَيْنَهُ، وَهُوَ مُحْرِمٌ ضَمَّدَهَا بِالصَّبِرِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Sufyan ibn 'Uyaynah -> Ayyub ibn Musa -> Nubayh ibn Wahb -> Aban ibn 'Uthman
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Sufyan ibn 'Uyaynah narrated to us from Ayyub ibn Musa, from Nubayh ibn Wahb, from Aban ibn 'Uthman, that he informed him that 'Uthman narrated from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ regarding a man who suffers from an eye ailment while in Ihram: "He bandages it with aloe."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ بْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنْ عُمَرَ، أَنَّهُ فَعَلَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Sufyan ibn 'Uyaynah -> Nafi' -> 'Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Sufyan ibn 'Uyaynah narrated to us from Nafi', from 'Umar, "that he did it."
